level_signals: Comp range $365K-$435K base, consistent with Senior IC at Anthropic
'Several years of experience in technical program management' phrasing
Cross-org, end-to-end platform ownership scope implies significant seniority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have several years of experience in technical program management, with a track record of successfully delivering complex, cross-functional programs

Have experience with platform engineering efforts (shared services, developer platforms, or common capabilities consumed by multiple product teams) and can engage meaningfully with the engineers building them
Have driven platform adoption across consuming teams, where success depended on other teams choosing to build on what you shipped
Have experience coordinating across multiple engineering teams and organizations simultaneously
Are highly organized and can manage multiple parallel workstreams effectively across distributed teams
Thrive in unstructured environments with a knack for bringing order to chaos and creating clarity in ambiguous situations
Have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence without authority
Are passionate about Anthropic's mission and interested in the challenges of bringing AI capabilities to users safely and reliably
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

scope.surface: Apps Platform — shared platform capabilities powering Claude, Cowork, Claude Code, and future surfaces
scope.business_model: Internal developer platform serving consumer and developer products; B2C and B2B end products
primary_metrics_inferred: Platform adoption rate by product teams
Speed and reliability of product shipping on shared platform
core_responsibilities: Own the cross-Apps platform roadmap end to end: sequencing, dependencies, and tradeoffs across platform teams and the apps teams that depend on them
Drive alignment between platform engineering and apps teams so platform investments map to real consumer needs and adoption actually happens
Identify duplication across surfaces and convert it into shared platform capabilities
Establish the operating rhythm: planning, status, escalation paths, and launch coordination across orgs
Create program documentation including roadmaps, status reports, risk assessments, and communication plans
Facilitate technical decision-making by bringing together stakeholders, driving consensus, and ensuring timely resolution of blocking issues

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($365K-$435K base)
Clear product surface and ownership area — not a vague TPM role
Explicit encouragement to apply even without meeting every qualification
Cross-org scope signals high-impact, high-visibility role
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Years of experience floor is unstated ('several years') — ambiguous bar to assess against
Single city (San Francisco) implied from HQ reference but not explicitly named for this role in the JD body — minor ambiguity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + scope; not stated explicitly)
seniority.years_required_min (not stated; 'several years' used in JD but no number given)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (inferred as San Francisco from HQ reference; body does not name a specific city for this role)
ambiguities: 'Several years of experience' is used rather than a specific number; years_required_min left null
No explicit nice-to-haves section in the JD; all 'You may be a good fit if you' bullets treated as must-haves per hard classification rules since they appear as unconditional criteria
Location city not explicitly named in role body; San Francisco inferred from company HQ context
